steps.title: Compare the given angles
steps.content:
Triangle $ABC$ and triangle $XYZ$ already have two matching angle pairs. Angle $A$ matches angle $X$, and angle $B$ matches angle $Y$:
$$A=X=35^\circ$$
$$B=Y=85^\circ$$

steps.title: Apply triangle similarity
steps.content:
Two equal angle pairs are enough to prove triangles similar by angle-angle similarity. If desired, the third angles are also forced to match because each triangle has angle sum $180^\circ$:
$$180^\circ-35^\circ-85^\circ=60^\circ$$So no side lengths or third-angle measures are needed. The answer is D.
